description: Video recommendation algorithm based on readiness assessment results for Chapter 14: Data Handling (Tally marks, Pictographs, Bar Graphs)

excellent_performance.rationale: Student has excellent counting, sorting, and comparison skills. Can fast-track through data collection and move quickly to graphs and interpretation

videoSequence.reason: Introduction to collecting and organising data
Learn tally marks for recording data
Learn to read and draw bar graphs
Practice making bar graphs from data tables
Challenge: Interpret and compare data from graphs

ready_performance.rationale: Student is ready. Provide complete coverage of data handling from collection through interpretation

videoSequence.reason: Learn to collect and organise data
Learn tally marks for counting data
Learn to read and draw bar graphs
Practice making bar graphs from data
Read and interpret data from graphs

partial_readiness.rationale: Student has gaps in counting, comparison, or number reading. Needs targeted review before data handling concepts

adaptiveLogic.description: Select prerequisite videos based on specific weak concepts

exampleSequence.reason: Review: Counting and sorting objects into groups
Review: Comparison vocabulary (more, less, most, least)
Review: Reading numbers on a number line
Start main topic: Collecting and organising data
Reading and interpreting data from graphs

not_ready.rationale: Student needs significant review in counting, sorting, and comparison before attempting data handling

videoSequence.reason: Foundation: Review counting and sorting into groups
Essential: Learn comparison words (more, less, most, least)
Critical: Reading numbers and understanding number values
Gentle intro to collecting and organising data
Begin main content: Tally marks for counting

postPlaylistAction.message: After watching these prerequisite videos, retake the readiness quiz to check if you are ready to continue with data handling.

conceptBasedRemediation.description: Map specific weak concepts to remedial videos

counting_sorting.conceptName: Counting objects and sorting them into groups

comparison_vocabulary.conceptName: Using comparison words like more, less, most, and least

reading_numbers.conceptName: Reading numbers and understanding number values up to 100

basic_addition.conceptName: Adding small numbers to find totals

comprehensionCheckpoints.description: Quick checks during video watching to adjust playlist

questions.question: How many tally marks does this show: |||| || ?
questions.explanation: The first group of 5 tally marks (with the line across) equals 5, plus 2 more marks = 7.

questions.question: If 8 children like cricket, how many tally marks would you draw?
questions.explanation: 8 tally marks = one group of 5 (||||) plus 3 more (|||) = |||| |||.

ifAllCorrect.message: Excellent! You understand tally marks perfectly.

ifAllWrong.reason: Student struggling with tally marks, review counting and grouping

questions.question: In a bar graph, if the bar for 'Mango' reaches up to 10 and the bar for 'Apple' reaches up to 6, which fruit do more children like?
questions.explanation: The taller bar means more children chose that option. Mango's bar is at 10, which is more than Apple's bar at 6.

ifCorrect.reason: Student ready for data interpretation challenge

ifWrong.message: Watch the bar graph video again and pay attention to how the height of each bar shows the number.

skip_ahead.action: Skip practice video, jump to challenge or next topic

add_support.action: Pause main topic, insert prerequisite review on counting and comparison

suggest_break.trigger: After 4 videos watched in a single session
suggest_break.action: Suggest taking a 5-minute break before continuing

recommend_practice.trigger: Student completes videos but scores < 70% on checkpoint
recommend_practice.action: Recommend practising data collection and tally marks with real objects at home

recommend_retake.trigger: Student in not_ready category completes all prerequisite videos
recommend_retake.action: Strongly recommend retaking readiness assessment before main content

high_score.message: Excellent! You are great at counting, sorting, and comparing. You are ready to learn about data handling. We have created a fast-track playlist with all the exciting graph topics for you.

good_score.message: Good work! You understand counting and comparing well. Let's learn how to collect data and show it using tally marks, pictographs, and bar graphs.

partial_score.message: You have some gaps in counting, comparison, or number reading. Don't worry! We will start with targeted review videos on the concepts you need, then move to data handling.

low_score.message: You need to review counting, sorting, and comparison first. We have created a playlist focused on building a strong foundation. After watching, you can retake the quiz and then learn about graphs and charts.

conceptProgressionMap.description: Shows how data handling concepts build on each other

playlistCompletion.description: % of students who watch >= 80% of recommended videos

practiceQuizPassRate.description: % of students who score >= 70% on practice quiz after watching playlist

videoRelevanceRating.description: Average student rating (out of 5) for playlist helpfulness

timeToMastery.description: Average minutes from readiness quiz to practice quiz pass

tallyMarksFluency.description: % of students who can correctly read and write tally marks

graphReadingAccuracy.description: % of students who can correctly read data values from a bar graph or pictograph

implementationNotes.recommendedValidation: Test with 15-20 Grade 4 students across all readiness levels

expectedOutcomes.excellent_students: Complete in 40-50 minutes with 90-100% practice quiz score
expectedOutcomes.ready_students: Complete in 45-55 minutes with 80-90% practice quiz score
expectedOutcomes.partial_students: Complete in 55-65 minutes with 70-80% practice quiz score (after prerequisite review)
expectedOutcomes.not_ready_students: Require prerequisite review (20 min) + retake + main content (30 min) = 50-60 total minutes, expected 60-75% on practice quiz

commonChallenges.challenge1: Students confusing tally marks grouping (forgetting to cross at 5)
commonChallenges.solution1: Emphasise the bundle of 5 pattern. Use physical sticks or straws to demonstrate grouping by 5s.
commonChallenges.challenge2: Students unable to read the scale on a pictograph (each picture = 2 or 5)
commonChallenges.solution2: Always point out the key/legend first. Practice counting by 2s and 5s before reading pictographs.
commonChallenges.challenge3: Students drawing bar graph bars of incorrect height
commonChallenges.solution3: Use grid paper so students can count squares. Start with scales of 1 before moving to scales of 2 or 5.
